title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Applied Materials (AMAT) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 12% growth rate is robust, reflecting the massive multi-year fab buildouts driven by the CHIPS Act globally and insatiable demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and logic chips. AMAT's portfolio uniquely positions it to capture an outsized share of this rising WFE spend.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: <div class="assumption-grid" data-astro-cid-re4dfcsi> <div class="assumption-card" data-astro-cid-re4dfcsi> <div class="card-title" data-astro-cid-re4dfcsi>FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.0% represents long-term, steady-state growth slightly above global GDP. Silicon is becoming the foundational commodity of the modern economy, ensuring perpetual, if mature, growth for the underlying manufacturing ecosystem.

risks.introText: To justify a long position near current price levels, you must believe the market is severely underpricing a sustained, multi-year supercycle. Here are the core risks to the downside:

items.title: 1. China Export Restrictions
items.description: The U.g. government has increasingly tightened export controls on advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China. Given that China represents a significant portion of historic revenue for equipment makers, further restrictions could immediately dent top-line growth and cash flow.

items.title: 2. Foundry Capex Delays
items.description: If major customers like Intel delay their ambitious fab buildouts (e.g., due to internal financial pressures or slower-than-expected macro demand), AMAT's revenue recognition will stall. The stock price currently assumes near-perfect execution of these global fab roadmaps.

items.title: 3. Valuation Multiple Compression
items.description: The entire semiconductor space is trading at historically elevated multiples due to the AI boom. If market sentiment shifts or if the return on investment (ROI) for downstream AI applications fails to materialize quickly, multiples will compress severely, driving the stock price down even if AMAT executes perfectly.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).
